prompt: A wide-angle landscape shot of an open, grassy public park. A paved concrete walking path cuts across the grass, but instead of a smooth curve, the path violently zig-zags, loops, and juts out in sharp, erratic, chaotic angles. At the exact corner of every bizarre zig-zag on the path sits a single random object: a discarded paper cup, a stray rock, or a single weed. The heavy concrete path goes wildly out of its way to physically intersect every single piece of random debris on the lawn, creating a hyper-complex, jagged, absurd walkway that ignores the natural flow of the landscape. Photorealistic, bright daylight, architectural photography. No people, no text, no signs, no diagrams, no floating labels.
metaphor: A paved park path that violently zig-zags to intersect every random rock and piece of trash on the lawn, perfectly capturing the 'noise' rather than the underlying trend.
